title: Anti-Flood / Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: The anti-flood safety system has detected water in the base tray of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: Error E01 on Candy dishwashers indicates the float switch in the base tray has been triggered by water leaking into the bottom. The machine stops water intake and runs the drain pump continuously to prevent flooding.
symptoms: Machine will not start a cycle

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain base
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and turn off water supply. Tilt machine backward 45 degrees to drain the base tray.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door gasket for tears, deformation, or buildup. Clean or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Remove side panels and inspect all hose connections for drips, especially at the wash pump and drain pump.

fixSteps.title: Identify and fix leak
fixSteps.instruction: Run a short fill cycle to identify the exact leak source. Replace the faulty gasket, clamp, or component.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source involves the wash pump or sump.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E01 anti flood leak fix

faq.q: Can I just dry out the base?
faq.a: Tilting clears the error temporarily, but the leak will cause it to recur. Find and fix the leak source.

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause E01?
faq.a: Yes, excessive suds can overflow through seals into the base tray.

faq.q: How does the float switch work?
faq.a: A polystyrene float rises when water enters the base, triggering a microswitch that alerts the control board.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher has not filled with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the dishwasher did not reach the required water level within the timeout period. Usually a water supply issue rather than a machine fault.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the tap is fully open and water is flowing.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose and filter
fixSteps.instruction: Check for kinks. Disconnect and clean the filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check for voltage during fill attempt. If voltage present but no water, valve is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect hose, swap valve.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E02 water fill error fix

faq.a: Yes, very low water pressure prevents timely filling.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet filter?
faq.a: Disconnect the hose and pull the mesh screen out with pliers. Rinse under water.
A safety valve in the hose that shuts off if the hose bursts or leaks.

title: Drain Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not drain the water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the drain cycle did not complete in time. Water remains in the sump. Typically caused by filter blockages, drain hose obstructions, or a failed drain pump.
symptoms: Drain pump sounds strained or silent

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filter components from the dishwasher floor.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks and blockages. Ensure proper high loop.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Remove filter housing and check impeller for obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If pump motor has failed, replace the pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E03 drain blocked fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: After every 3-5 cycles. Always scrape plates before loading.

faq.q: Can the sink drain cause E03?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked waste pipe prevents drainage.
Prevents dirty water from siphoning back into the dishwasher.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor is out of range or disconnected.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the NTC thermistor has failed or is reading outside its expected range. The dishwasher cannot control water temperature for proper washing and sanitization.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Locate NTC in the sump area. Measure resistance (10K-50K ohms at room temp).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wiring from NTC to control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Pull old sensor, insert new one, reconnect.

whenToCallPro: Only if NTC and wiring test fine.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E04 temperature sensor fix

faq.q: What temperature should the dishwasher reach?
faq.a: 55-70°C depending on cycle. Eco cycles run at about 45-50°C.
The machine will run but cleaning will be very poor.
No, NTC sensors typically cost only 5-18.

title: Circulation Pump Fault
shortDescription: The wash circulation pump is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error E05 indicates the circulation pump that drives water through the spray arms is not running or is jammed. Without water circulation, dishes cannot be cleaned.

causes.cause: Pump motor seized or burned out

symptoms: Machine fills and drains but does not wash

fixSteps.title: Check spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and ensure they spin freely. Clear blocked holes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Access from underneath and check for debris.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor continuity (3-15 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: If motor failed, replace the circulation pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: Circulation pump replacement involves significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E05 wash pump error fix

faq.a: Yes, glass fragments can jam the pump impeller.

faq.q: Is this the same as the drain pump?
faq.a: No, the circulation pump is larger and powers the spray arms.

faq.q: What does the circulation pump do?
faq.a: It forces water at high pressure through the spray arms to clean dishes.

title: Heating Element Fault
shortDescription: The wash water heating element has failed or has a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates the heating element is open circuit, shorted to ground, or the control board cannot power it. Hot water is essential for effective dishwashing.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (15-30 ohms) and insulation (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect heater terminals for corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace heater
fixSteps.instruction: Drain water, remove old element, install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the heater trips the RCD or the board relay is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E06 heater element replacement

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water?
faq.a: Cleaning will be very poor. Grease and tablets need hot water.
A heater leaking electricity to ground trips the safety switch.

faq.q: How long does a heater last?
faq.a: Typically 7-12 years. Hard water areas may see earlier failure.

title: EEPROM / Control Board Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM has corrupted data or the board has a general failure.
fullDescription: Error E07 indicates the EEPROM memory chip on the control board has corrupted data, or the board has a general processing failure. The machine cannot run cycle programs correctly.

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes for full reset.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Open the machine and reseat all board connectors.

fixSteps.title: EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: A technician may reprogram the EEPROM.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming fails, replace the board.

whenToCallPro: E07 almost always requires professional intervention.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E07 EEPROM control board fix

faq.a: Power outages during write operations and voltage spikes.
A board at 65-150 is worthwhile on machines under 8-10 years old.
Yes, ensure the exact part number for your model.

title: Spray Arm / Diverter Fault
shortDescription: The spray arm diverter motor or mechanism is not working correctly.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates the diverter that alternates water between upper and lower spray arms has failed. This results in poor cleaning on one or both racks.

fixSteps.title: Check spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and ensure they spin freely. Clear blocked holes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect diverter
fixSteps.instruction: Check the diverter at the sump for debris. Clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Test diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance and check for voltage during operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace diverter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and replace the diverter assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the main wash pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E08 diverter spray arm fix

faq.a: It alternates water between upper and lower spray arms for even cleaning.

faq.q: Why is only the top rack clean?
faq.a: The diverter may be stuck directing water to one arm only.
Items blocking spray arms can overload the diverter mechanism.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: The display module and main control board cannot communicate properly.
fullDescription: Error E09 indicates a communication fault between the user interface and the main board. This can be caused by a loose cable, failed display board, or moisture damage.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open door panel and reseat the cable between display and main board.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for corrosion. Clean with electronics cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes after reseating.

fixSteps.title: Replace display board
fixSteps.instruction: Start with the less expensive display board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating does not fix it.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E09 communication error fix

faq.a: Yes, steam corrodes connectors over time.
Start with the cable, then the display board.
Keep door seal in good condition. Avoid opening mid-cycle when hot.

title: Water Level / Turbidity Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water level or turbidity sensor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: Error E10 indicates the water level sensor or the turbidity sensor (which measures water cleanliness) is providing out-of-range signals. Some models use a turbidity sensor to optimize cycle length and water usage.

causes.cause: Turbidity sensor covered in grease or food

symptoms: Water level too high or too low

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The sensor is usually in the sump area. Remove filter assembly and clean the optical sensor lens with a soft cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: If there is a pressure switch, verify the hose is connected and not blocked.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test according to service manual specifications.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If sensor cleaning does not resolve the issue.
videoSearchQuery: Candy dishwasher E10 turbidity sensor fix

faq.a: An optical sensor that measures how dirty the wash water is by checking light transmission through it.

faq.q: Does cleaning the sensor usually fix E10?
faq.a: Yes, in many cases cleaning grease and film off the sensor lens resolves the error.

faq.q: Do all Candy dishwashers have a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: No, only higher-end models. Budget models use simpler pressure-based water level detection.
